description: This figure contains three subplots corresponding to Y=0, Y=1 and Y=2 positions respectively, presenting the spatial distribution of Young's modulus (unit: GPa) of pre-deformed SPCC/SPCE low carbon steel near grain boundaries as a function of position X (unit: μm). Two spherical nanoindentation tips with radius R=50μm and R=200μm are used for the tests, marked with different symbols: pink squares and dark blue diamonds for R=50μm, yellow triangles for R=200μm. A consistent trend is observed across all subplots: the measured Young's modulus generally decreases as X increases from grain interior to grain boundary; measurements with the larger R=200μm tip, which samples a larger volume containing more grain boundary areas with dislocation pile-ups, yield 50-150 GPa lower modulus values than those from the smaller R=50μm tip. These results confirm that local Young's modulus reduction near grain boundaries induced by dislocation pile-up after plastic deformation is the micro-mechanism for the macroscopic elastic modulus decrease with plastic strain, providing experimental basis for improved spring-back FEM simulation accuracy.
